novel_features.name: Body-recomposition tracker
novel_features.description: See whether you're actually recomposing — fat mass down while lean mass holds — on a de-noised rolling-average weight, not scale-weight whiplash.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join across weight, fat-mass and lean-mass measures over a window; no single getmeas call produces the fat-down/muscle-held divergence.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this instead of raw measures when the question is 'is my cut/bulk working', not 'what did I weigh on Tuesday'.

novel_features.name: Training load vs recovery
novel_features.description: Weigh recent workout HR-zone load against your recovery markers (resting HR, HRV, sleep score) to catch overtraining before it catches you.
novel_features.rationale: Joins workouts, measures and sleep summaries in the local store — three tables no Withings endpoint spans.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when deciding whether to push or back off; it correlates how hard you trained with how recovered you are.

novel_features.name: Clinician BP/AFib report
novel_features.description: A dated blood-pressure + AFib table with your own annotations (medication changes, symptoms) — the clean history to hand a cardiologist.
novel_features.rationale: Joins BP measures with ECG/AFib heart records plus a local annotations table; the app offers graphs, not an annotatable dated export.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for a doctor-ready BP/AFib history; prefer it over raw 'heart list' when you need the annotated report.

novel_features.name: Sleep-debt rolling window
novel_features.description: Cumulative sleep deficit against your target over a rolling window — the number the per-night summary never adds up for you.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates per-night sleep summaries across a window in the local store; getsummary only returns a single night.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for sleep-deficit accounting over time; for one night use 'sleep summary'.

novel_features.name: Since-time health digest
novel_features.description: One structured 'what changed since <time> across all my metrics' snapshot — built for piping into an agent.
novel_features.rationale: Reads every entity in the local mirror since a timestamp into one envelope; replaces a bespoke multi-call ETL.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The go-to for LLM health Q&A: one cursor-driven structured pull instead of N endpoint calls.

novel_features.name: Multi-metric correlation
novel_features.description: Pearson + best-lag correlation between any two daily metrics (weight vs sleep score, steps vs resting HR).
novel_features.rationale: Pulls two arbitrary daily series from the local mirror and correlates them; no endpoint offers cross-metric analysis.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The flexible fallback when no curated readout fits the metric pair you care about.

narrative.headline: Every Withings metric in one offline-first, agent-native CLI — with a local SQLite mirror and recomposition, recovery, sleep-debt and clinician-report analytics no Withings tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Pulls your weight/body-composition, activity, sleep, heart/ECG and workouts from the official Withings API into a local SQLite store you can search, query with SQL, and export as JSON or CSV. On top of that it adds local-join analytics — `recomp`, `recovery`, `bp-report`, `sleep debt`, `digest`, `correlate` — that no single API call can produce.
narrative.auth_narrative: Withings uses OAuth2. Register a free app at the Withings developer portal, run `withings-pp-cli auth login` once to authorize in your browser, and the CLI stores and auto-rotates your refresh token (Withings refresh tokens are single-use, so the CLI persists each new one for you).

quickstart.comment: Check config and reachability before anything else (works without auth).
Mirror the last 90 days into local SQLite.
See your real fat-vs-lean recomposition trend.
Structured 'what changed' snapshot for an agent.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `withings-pp-cli auth refresh` (or `auth login` to re-authorize); access tokens last 3h and the CLI rotates the single-use refresh token automatically.
Rate limit (120 req/min). Slow down or rely on local sync + offline queries instead of repeated live calls.
Confirm the resource was synced: `withings-pp-cli sync --resources measure` then `withings-pp-cli measure get --limit 5`.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you want programmatic, offline-queryable access to your own Withings health data and analytics across metrics over time — body recomposition, training recovery, sleep debt, BP/AFib reports for a clinician, or a structured health digest to feed an LLM. It is ideal for agents answering 'how is my health trending' and for scripted exports.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to push data to Garmin/TrainerRoad/Strava — use withings-sync for one-way fitness-platform sync.
Do not use it for medical diagnosis; it reports your recorded measurements and is not a clinical device.
Do not use it to access another person's Withings data — it operates only on the authorized account.

recipes.title: Mirror everything, then ask offline
recipes.explanation: One incremental sync populates the local store for fast offline analytics.

recipes.title: Agent-native health digest
recipes.explanation: Narrowed structured output an agent can consume without parsing the full envelope.

recipes.title: Doctor-ready BP/AFib history
recipes.explanation: A dated BP + AFib table with your medication-change annotation, exported as CSV.

recipes.title: Is my cut working?
recipes.explanation: De-noised weight trend plus fat-down/muscle-held verdict over the window.
